reviews.feedback:
"The best outcome is when an engineer I mentored ships something that ripples further than either of us could have reached alone.” this misses the point that I created an impact beyond just coding, via mentoring others. Explain that building effective and efficient engineering organization relies on tooling, coaching, mentoring, and building a collaborative and creative culture, not just writing code or meeting feature deadlines. It’s also coming up with creative solutions that solve many different problems at the same time - and staying in touch with actually doing the work.

I really like the last two paragraphs though, they are short and punchy.

The transition to “came to software by way of” is awkward and surprising in a bad way. The load-bearing feels forced. Let’s refocus this to say that he’s on his third career after medical school and heavy metal music to make light of it.
